_comment: Populated affordances for key Wang Family Village entities. Per Article XXVIII, each entity exposes verbs the UI and AI discover. This file covers the entities a player is most likely to interact with in Chapter 1. Remaining village entities (other NPCs, minor items) will be populated in subsequent cycles following the same schema.

affordances.label: Talk
affordances.description: Speak with Wang Ping. He may share what's on his mind — his desires fire conversationally, not on demand.
affordances.outcome: Wang Ping says a context-appropriate line based on his current desire_state and the memory ledger.
outcome_variants: If winter approaching and firewood low: asks for oak logs (request engine).
If dog recently died: goes quiet, then '...Used to have one.' (rumor/grief).
If Heng Yue recruiter present: asks you to pass the test (favor engine).
Default: invites fishing at the south bend (offer engine).

affordances.label: Go fishing with Wang Ping
affordances.description: Accompany Wang Ping and Da Niu to the south bend to fish. If a wolf attack is staged, you face it together.
affordances.outcome: Travel to south bend. Catch cod. If wolves spawn, the dog (if alive) defends; if the dog dies here, the canonical memory event fires.
outcome_variants: Wolf attack: dog may die (triggers staged_005 memory), Wang Ping traumatized.

affordances.label: Give oak logs for firewood
affordances.description: Fulfill Wang Ping's firewood request. Builds favor; prepares the family for winter.
affordances.outcome: Wang Ping gives coal (4). Favor +2. winter_preparedness +1. Memory: firewood_delivered.

affordances.label: Comfort Wang Ping (after the dog's death)
affordances.description: Sit with Wang Ping after the dog dies. He won't say much. Neither do you. That is enough.
affordances.outcome: Wang Ping's grief easing. Favor +1. Memory: comforted_wang_ping. His grief_dog_death desire cools sooner.

entities.entity_name: Wang Family Hunting Dog (Old Chen's dog)

affordances.label: Pet the dog
affordances.description: The dog wags its tail. A small thing.
affordances.outcome: +morale (tiny). The dog remembers you (affects its behavior if you return).

affordances.label: Feed the dog
affordances.description: Give the dog food. It follows you for a while after.
affordances.outcome: The dog follows you for 6000 ticks. If a wolf attacks while the dog follows you, it defends you — and may die in your defense instead of Old Chen's.
outcome_variants: If the dog dies defending you: the memory event attributes the death to your presence, not Old Chen's. Different witnesses, different retelling, different grief.

affordances.label: Command the dog to hunt
affordances.description: Send the dog after a spirit rabbit or fox. The dog may bring back prey — or may encounter wolves.
affordances.outcome: Dog hunts. 70% brings back prey. 20% nothing. 10% encounters wolves — may trigger the staged wolf attack if the dog is at the south bend.

affordances.label: Enter the childhood home
affordances.description: Step inside the home where Wang Lin grew up. Modest. A wooden table, a straw mat, a faded family mark on the wall.
affordances.outcome: You see the interior. If it is night and you are quiet, you may notice a faint spiritual fluctuation — the residue of the Heaven Defying Bead's proximity, though you cannot identify it without higher perception.
outcome_variants: Qi Condensation: 'Something feels... off. Like a memory of power.'
Foundation Establishment: 'There is a spiritual residue here. Old. Faint. Coming from the ancestral hall direction.'

affordances.label: Examine the family mark on the wall
affordances.description: Study the faded Wang family mark. It is older than it looks.
affordances.outcome: You realize the mark is not merely decorative — it is a faint, degraded formation fragment. The Wang family was once more than farmers. Knowledge +wang_family_history_fragment.

affordances.label: Enter the ancestral hall
affordances.description: The hall holds the Wang family genealogy and ancestral tablets. Quiet. Old. The air is thicker here.
affordances.outcome: You enter. If perception >= foundation_establishment, you sense the strange jade among the records (seed_006 memory). Otherwise, you see only old scrolls.

affordances.label: Search the genealogy records
affordances.description: Comb through the Wang family records. Time-consuming. May reveal history.
affordances.outcome: You find references to a cultivator ancestor three generations back — confirming the family mark fragment. You may (30%) notice the strange jade without the elder's gift.
outcome_variants: If you notice the jade: you may take it — but the village elder will know, and favor drops. Stealing has consequences.
If Wang Tianshui is present and favor>=3: he may explain the jade and gift it freely.

affordances.label: Take the strange jade (steal)
affordances.description: Take the jade without permission. It is theft. The village will know.
affordances.outcome: You obtain the Heaven Defying Bead (or fragment) — but as a thief, not a recipient. The village elder's trust is broken. Memory: stole_the_jade. Future mentorship from Wang Lin may be impossible — he judges motive (Article XXXI.2).
outcome_variants: If Wang Tianshui witnesses: confrontation. May escalate to violence (he is Qi Condensation).
If unobserved: the theft is discovered days later when the jade's absence is noted. Rumor spreads.

affordances.label: Receive the strange jade (gift)
affordances.description: Wang Tianshui gifts you the jade. No explanation. No quest. Just a gift.
affordances.outcome: You receive the jade. Wang Tianshui says nothing useful about it. The plot begins — but quietly. Memory: received_the_jade. Wang Lin (when encountered) may sense the bead on you and judge you worthy of mentorship — or unworthy, if you stole it.

entities.entity_name: The South Bend (fishing spot, wolf territory)

affordances.label: Fish at the south bend
affordances.description: Cast a line. The fishing is good here — better than the north bank. But it is far from the village, and wolves hunt these woods.
affordances.outcome: Catch cod (1-3). 5% chance (raised to 25% at night) of a wolf encounter. If the dog is with you, it may die defending — the canonical memory event.
outcome_variants: After dog_died_wolf_attack memory + 7 days: Old Chen's grief makes the bend feel heavier. No mechanical change, but NPCs avoid it.

affordances.label: Investigate the wolf tracks
affordances.description: You notice wolf tracks in the mud. Several. Recent.
affordances.outcome: Knowledge +south_bend_wolf_activity. You can warn the village (rumor engine) — if you do, the dog's death may be prevented. If you don't, it likely isn't.

affordances.label: Inspect the irrigation ditch
affordances.description: The ditch waters the village fields. It is cracked in places. The crops depend on it.
affordances.outcome: You see the state of the ditch. If damaged (world_state:irrigation_damaged=true), you may repair it. Wang Ping or Wang Tianshui may approach to help (their offer_labor / request_irrigation_repair desires).

affordances.label: Repair the irrigation ditch
affordances.description: Fix the cracks. Hard work. The village depends on it. If you do, Wang Tianshui owes you a debt.
affordances.outcome: Irrigation repaired. Village crop yield +1 (next harvest). Wang Tianshui favor +3, debt +1 (he may call it in later). Memory: repaired_irrigation. If Wang Ping's offer_labor fires, he joins and the time cost halves.
